question: What vessel inner diameter does Table 1.3-1 give for NuScale vs a typical PWR, and how does Table 5.3-1 further detail the NuScale RPV dimensions?

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which is surrounded by a containment vessel for safety. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, housing critical reactor systems and providing structural support, while its materials and fabrication adhere to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and containment of fission products.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within regulatory safety criteria throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, core design, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design with fewer components and lower power output per module compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for clarity.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch01_0041.title: Major Systems
nuscale_ch01_0041.summary: The section describes the digital control systems in the NuScale plant, emphasizing the independence between safety-related and non-safety-related systems, and the use of human factors analysis to design operator displays for effective monitoring and control. It outlines typical control room functions, the alarm management system, and mentions the Technical Support Center and Radioactive Waste Building, which handles the processing and shipment of radioactive waste.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components are conducted according to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.
